subject: English
title: Reading Rainbow
tagline: Read simple sentences and answer comprehension questions!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What do we call the people in a story?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Characters.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What do we call where and when a story happens?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Setting.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What is the problem in a story called?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Conflict.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What is the part where the story is solved called?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Resolution.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What do we call the person telling the story?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Narrator.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: What is the main message of a story called?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Theme.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What comes first in a story?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Beginning.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What comes last in a story?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Ending.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: What do we call a made-up story?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Fiction.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: What do we call a true story?
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Non-fiction.
